args.subject: Action Item from Team Meeting — Coordinate development start (Deadline: 2024-05-20)
args.body:
Hi David,

From the Team Meeting Minutes (May 17, 2024): Please coordinate with the development team to start the initial phase.

Required action (precise):
- Engage the development team, confirm scope for the initial phase, create a short plan outlining the first sprint's key tasks and owners, and confirm the start date with the team.

Deadline: 2024-05-20

Please reply to confirm you’ve started this and share the short plan (1–2 paragraphs or a bullet list) by the deadline. If you need help or resources, let me know.

Thanks,
Emma Johnson

args.subject: Action Item from Team Meeting — Send agenda to client (Deadline: 2024-05-21)
args.body:
Hi Linda,

From the Team Meeting Minutes (May 17, 2024): Please email the file 'meeting-agenda-2024-05-15.docx' to john.mitchell@gmail.com for review.

Required action (precise):
- Locate the file 'meeting-agenda-2024-05-15.docx' in the shared drive.
- Attach it to an email addressed to john.mitchell@gmail.com with a brief message: "Hi John — Please review the attached meeting agenda and share any comments by the deadline." Include any relevant context if needed.
- Send the email and confirm by replying to me with the sent time and a copy of the message.

Deadline: 2024-05-21

Please confirm once sent or if you need help locating the file.

Thanks,
Emma Johnson

args.subject: Action Item from Team Meeting — Review sales projections (Deadline: 2024-05-19)
args.body:
Hi Mark,

From the Team Meeting Minutes (May 17, 2024): Please review the sales projections and provide feedback.

Required action (precise):
- Review the 'Sales Projections' section in the presentation or related document (check 'presentation-slides-2024-05-20.pptx' if needed).
- Provide detailed feedback on assumptions, figures, and any recommendations to adjust forecasts. Highlight any data points that need clarification and propose changes if necessary.

Deadline: 2024-05-19

Please reply with your feedback (bullet points or a short report). Let me know if you need access to supporting data.

Thanks,
Emma Johnson
